title: Docker Compose infrastructure
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.yml defines two services: postgres (image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16) and redis (image: redis:7-alpine)

postgres service: port 5432:5432, named volume trading_pgdata, env vars POSTGRES_DB=trading POSTGRES_USER=trading POSTGRES_PASSWORD=trading, healthcheck using pg_isready -U trading every 10s with 5 retries
redis service: port 6379:6379, named volume trading_redis_data, healthcheck using redis-cli ping every 10s with 5 retries
Both volumes declared under top-level volumes: section
docker compose up -d followed by docker compose ps shows both services as (healthy) within 60 seconds
docker compose down -v cleanly removes both containers and volumes
No hardcoded passwords in compose file — use environment variable references or accept the dev-only defaults as documented in .env.example
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
